simonetta.name: Simonetta Salamano
simonetta.bio: Graduated in Architecture from the Politecnico di Torino, she has been practicing professionally since 1990. She specializes in interior design and concept development, with particular attention to spatial layout studies and the quality of living environments. She accompanies clients in their choices of materials, colors, lighting, and furnishings, directly following the most creative phases of the project and offering personalized support including selections at showrooms and suppliers. She has deepened her expertise in renovation, with experience in residential, educational, healthcare, banking, and corporate environments, with an approach oriented toward the balance between functionality and expressiveness.

paola.name: Paola Ferro
paola.bio: Graduated in Architecture from the Politecnico di Torino, she has been practicing professionally since 1991. She specializes in architectural and urban design, construction management, and coordination of design processes, with consolidated experience in relations with public entities and private clients. She follows the operational phases of the project and construction site, coordinating contracts, companies, and professionals through to the final delivery of works. Her approach is oriented toward construction quality, control of the implementation process, and attention to themes of sustainability, energy efficiency, and living comfort.

villa-in-collina.title: Hillside Villa
villa-in-collina.description: A detached villa arranged over four levels, distinguished by a strong visual continuity between its spaces. The ground floor is organized around a pass-through fireplace - the focal point of the living-kitchen area and the central element of the entire project - capable of defining and simultaneously connecting the rooms. Natural light is an integral part of the home: it filters through every opening via the garden vegetation, establishing a constant dialogue between interior and exterior. The sleeping quarters, more secluded in character, are distributed across the two upper levels, ensuring privacy and tranquility. The interior palette draws upon the colors of the surrounding landscape, reinforcing the harmony between the house and its natural setting.

attico-in-centro.title: Penthouse in the City Center
attico-in-centro.description: A panoramic residence in the heart of Turin, overlooking the rooftops and church bell towers of the city. The intervention involved a complete renovation, enhancing the generous glazed surfaces that ensure intense and constant luminosity throughout the day. The kitchen was designed with a semi-transparent sliding partition connecting it to the living room, creating a continuous dialogue between the spaces. An environment conceived to accommodate the owner's principal passion, without sacrificing elegance or functionality. The living room is configured as a welcoming setting, ideal for entertaining guests and for enjoying the evocative panorama over the city.

appartamento-palazzo-600.title: Apartment in a 17th Century Building
appartamento-palazzo-600.description: An elegant late-seventeenth-century apartment in the heart of Turin, with dual exposure onto two of the city's historic avenues. The tall coffered ceilings, present throughout every room, guided the design choices: in the bathroom and corridor, targeted lowered sections were introduced to accommodate the ducted air conditioning system, integrated with discretion into the historic context. Thanks to the skilled work of the parquet craftsmen, the original flooring was salvaged, mended with new planks, and extended seamlessly throughout the entire residence, restoring continuity and material richness to the spaces. Light is the protagonist of the generous salon, enhanced by four French doors opening onto balconies. Particular care was devoted to the study of artificial lighting, carefully calibrated to dialogue with the dark tonality of the timber ceiling and to ensure visual balance and comfort.

appartamento-panoramico.title: Panoramic Apartment
appartamento-panoramico.description: A panoramic apartment situated on an upper floor of a recently constructed building, characterized by a strong visual relationship with the surrounding landscape. The design favored the creation of a generous open space dedicated to the living area, where kitchen and sitting room converse harmoniously with the entrance and study. The rooms, while maintaining their own functional identity, are conceived to offer a visual continuity that extends all the way to the profile of the Alpine arc, framed by the large glazed surfaces. The kitchen echoes, in its materials and finishes, the dominant colors of the furnishings, contributing to a balanced and coherent whole. The design choices prioritized linearity, luminosity, and formal clarity, elevating natural light and the panorama as central elements of the living experience.
alts: Panoramic open-plan living area with Alpine views, Panoramic Apartment - expansive glazing and mountain skyline
Kitchen with coordinated materials and finishes, Panoramic Apartment - balanced design in contemporary apartment
Living room with full-height sliding doors, Panoramic Apartment - fluid spatial perception and verticality

attico-con-terrazzo.title: Penthouse with Terrace
attico-con-terrazzo.description: This apartment is located on the eighth floor of a nineteen-seventies building and is characterized by a generous terrace and large east-facing glazed openings that afford a pleasant luminosity during the morning hours. To meet the owners' needs, the original separate kitchen was removed and a single living environment was created, incorporating a kitchenette and dining area directly accessible from the entrance. The new spatial configuration privileges openness and conviviality, making the living zone the heart of the home. Through targeted plasterboard ceiling drops, the various functions were visually separated without interrupting the continuity of the space. The false ceiling interventions also enabled the integration of a ducted air conditioning system, with diffusers distributed throughout every room, ensuring comfort and aesthetic uniformity.
alts: Open-plan living area with kitchenette, Penthouse with terrace - unified living space on the eighth floor
East-facing windows with morning light, Penthouse with terrace - generous glazing and terrace views
Ceiling detail with plasterboard drops, Penthouse with terrace - zoning without partitions and integrated climate system
Dining area in renovated open space, Penthouse with terrace - contemporary conviviality and functional design

mini-appartamento.title: Mini Apartment
mini-appartamento.description: A renovation of a compact 35-square-meter studio apartment, situated on the ground floor with access from the inner courtyard. The partially shaded position made a careful study of the artificial lighting system essential, conceived as a key element of the project. The vaulted ceiling was lowered above the kitchen area, creating a dual lighting effect: focused, functional illumination in the working zone and softer, more diffuse light toward the living area, thus amplifying the perception of space. In the bathroom, of extremely limited dimensions, every element was custom designed: a shower with an irregular geometry, a trapezoidal vanity top with an oval basin, and a WC with a hand shower. A meticulous work of interlocking forms and proportions made it possible to optimize every available centimeter. A small space, yet fully functional, where compactness and comfort coexist in equilibrium.
alts: Vaulted living area with dual lighting design, Mini Apartment - maximized spatial perception in 35 sqm
Compact kitchen under lowered vault, Mini Apartment - focused functional illumination
Custom-designed bathroom with irregular shower, Mini Apartment - bespoke solutions for compact dimensions
Living zone with diffuse ambient lighting, Mini Apartment - elegant ground-floor renovation
Interior detail showing vaulted ceiling and lighting, Mini Apartment - compactness and comfort in equilibrium

casa-di-campagna.title: Country House
casa-di-campagna.description: An old country house, uninhabited for decades and in need of significant structural rehabilitation. It was necessary to undertake the consolidation of the building, including the reconstruction of the roof and the reinforcement of the floor slabs, restoring safety and stability to the entire architectural organism. Despite its complete functional transformation, the distinctive features of the original house were preserved and enhanced: the barrel vaults, the small vaulted ceilings with steel beams, and the reinforcement tie rods - elements that narrate the history of the building and define its material identity. Distributed over two levels with a garden at the front, the residence was reimagined to maintain the authentic character of a country house while combining it with a functional organization of spaces and contemporary comfort. Particular attention was devoted to conviviality, creating welcoming and flexible environments capable of hosting the owners' many friends.
alts: Restored barrel vault interior, Country House - historic structural elements preserved in renovation
Living area with original steel beams and vaulted ceiling, Country House - material identity and contemporary comfort
Kitchen in renovated country house, Piedmont - authentic rural character with modern functionality

scala-interna-studio-professionale.title: Internal Staricase in a Private Office
scala-interna-studio-professionale.description: The intervention focused on the creation of a new connecting staircase between the two floors designated as offices, bringing into relation spaces characterized by different pre-existing conditions. The staircase, with a steel structure and timber treads, is defined by a handrail that follows the profile of the steps and integrates an LED system, combining functionality with formal essentiality. Along the staircase, a cluster suspension lighting arrangement welcomes the client and contributes to perceptually reducing the height of the existing ceiling, making the environment more intimate and representative.
alts: Steel and timber staircase with LED handrail, Internal Staricase in a Private Office  Turin - functional design connecting two office floors
Cluster suspension lighting along staircase, Internal Staricase in a Private Office  Turin - intimate atmosphere and perceptual ceiling reduction

hair-stylist.title: Hair Stylist
hair-stylist.description: The intervention concerned the expansion of a shop in Turin, with the creation of a new reception area and a dedicated coat storage system equipped with a rotating garment rack. The project explored variations in floor level and ceiling inclinations, producing a dynamic interplay of heights. A pronounced ceiling drop marks the transition between the entrance-reception zone and the operational area, clearly defining the functions and guiding the client through the interior journey.
alts: New reception area with dynamic ceiling design, Hair Stylist - commercial expansion with architectural character
Rotating coat storage system, Hair Stylist - innovative functional solution in retail environment
Transition between reception and operational zones, Hair Stylist - pronounced ceiling drop defining spatial functions
Interior view with varied ceiling heights, Hair Stylist - dynamic interplay of levels and inclinations
Entrance area guiding the client journey, Hair Stylist - commercial interior design by Salamano & Ferro

stabilimento-industriale.title: Industrial Facility
stabilimento-industriale.description: The expansion project for the industrial facility involved the construction of approximately 6,800 square meters of new covered area, in continuity with the existing building. The intervention is situated on a 17,000-square-meter lot, part of a larger sector originally consisting of a flat green area. A portion was allocated to public green space and parking, integrating the productive expansion with amenities serving the community. The focal point of the intervention was the creation of an overhead pallet transport system, connected to the new automated warehouse - a strategic element for streamlining the logistics cycle. The new spaces were designed with the objective of optimizing production processes, with particular attention to safety and functionality. The self-supporting warehouse, clad in insulated sheet-metal panels, was deliberately differentiated to visually highlight the specificity of the activities housed within and to render the overall organization of the industrial complex legible.

appartamento-sul-corso.title: Apartment on a Street
appartamento-sul-corso.description: Spacious apartment located on the first floor of a 1960s building, boasting a southern exposure and characterized by a particularly evocative light that filters through the large trees of the boulevard in front during the summer months. Fulfilling the owners' wishes, the original marble flooring in the entrance was preserved, embellished with a 'Verde Alpi' edging, a signature element that introduces the home’s character. Taking advantage of the generous square footage of this area, two distribution corridors were created to functionally separate the sleeping quarters from the service area, which includes the kitchen and a study. Floor-to-ceiling sliding doors were installed to emphasize the verticality of the rooms while contributing to a fluid perception of the spaces. One of these, when slid open, reveals a hidden bookcase discreetly integrated into the interior architecture. Several antique objects, carefully maintained and highlighted, embellish the rooms and establish a balanced dialogue between memory and modernity.

cookie.message: This site uses technical cookies to improve your browsing experience.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy describes how the personal data of users visiting interniarchitettura.it is processed, in accordance with EU Regulation 2016/679 (GDPR).

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.content: The Data Controller is Ferro & Salamano Architetti studio, based in Turin, Italy. For any requests regarding personal data processing, please write to interni2architettura@gmail.com.

dataCollected.title: Data Collected
dataCollected.content: Through the contact form on this website, we collect the following personal data voluntarily provided by the user:

purpose.title: Purpose of Processing
purpose.content: The data collected through the contact form is used exclusively to respond to user inquiries and to provide information about the studio's services.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis
legalBasis.content: Data processing is based on the user's consent, expressed through the voluntary submission of the contact form (Art. 6, par. 1, letter a of GDPR).

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.content: Personal data will be retained for the time necessary to respond to the request and in any case no longer than 12 months from the last contact, unless required by law.

rights.title: User Rights
rights.content: Users have the right to access their data, request its rectification or deletion, restrict processing, object to processing, and request data portability. To exercise these rights, simply send a communication to interni2architettura@gmail.com.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.content: This website uses only technical cookies necessary for the site's operation. No profiling or third-party advertising cookies are used.

thirdParties.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParties.content: The website uses the following third-party services:
